ops.title: spawnllm isolated-spawn auth needs >=0.5.4 on cc-pool machines (Keychain fallback); judge tier large=opus matches the verdict corpus; enrich routes to codex by 'review' specialty

ops.body:
**Isolated-spawn auth: require spawnllm >= 0.5.4.** spawnllm runs every call with
`isolated=True` (a fresh temp `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`). Before 0.5.4 it seeded auth only
from `~/.claude/.credentials.json` — a stale expired relic on the cc-pool machine —
so every spawned call exited 1 with `401 Invalid authentication credentials` **on
stdout** (stderr empty; `run_verdicts` swallows it → "judged 0 rows (N failed)" with
zero diagnostics). Fixed upstream in **v0.5.4** (`75f8a04`, 2026-07-02): seeds from
the effective `$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` and falls back to the
`Claude Code-credentials-<sha256(home)[:8]>` macOS Keychain item. If a venv shows
this failure shape, check `uv pip show spawnllm` FIRST and
`uv lock --upgrade-package spawnllm` — do not re-diagnose (verified working
2026-07-03 with ambient cc-pool config home, no env hacks).

Fallback if ever pinned below 0.5.4: extract a pool account's accessToken from its
Keychain item (`ccp list` maps acct → service) into `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, which
overrides config-dir auth in spawned CLIs. Do NOT overwrite
`~/.claude/.credentials.json` from the Keychain (its `expiresAt: 0` reads as
not-logged-in to the file loader, and reusing the personal refreshToken outside the
Keychain risks rotation logging the user out). A `.expired-20260703` backup of that
relic file sits beside it.

**Spend routing:** the fixed seeding honors ambient `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`, so prefix
LLM passes with `eval "$(ccp env)"` (emptiest account) to keep judge spend off the
account running the interactive session.

**Judge model tier:** the v5 verdict corpus is keyed `model=opus` = `--model large`
(`medium`→sonnet would double-judge everything under a different model key).

**Backend routing differs by stage.** The judge/refine call `default_backend()` =
`select_backend()` with no specialty → **claude** (general). But enrich's
correction-picker calls `usable_backend()` = `select_backend(specialty="review")`,
which the registry routes to **codex** (`LLM_BACKENDS = {debugging: codex, review:
codex, general: claude}`). So enrich needs `codex` separately authed (`codex login`);
`codex login status` can say "Logged in using ChatGPT" while the refresh token is
revoked (real calls 401). Verify with a one-row extraction, not the status command.

**PATH (older gotcha, still applies in bare non-interactive shells):** `claude` must
resolve or `select_backend()` silently falls back to codex → empty verdicts. This
machine resolves via `~/.superset/bin/claude`; elsewhere prefix
`PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"`.

**Structured output is a single result-envelope.** The CLI emits one
`{"type":"result", ..., "structured_output":{...}}` object and the backend's
`parse_response(stdout, model)` validates it. The claude backend is stdout-based; the
codex backend (`codex exec`) writes its result to a `-o` file (mismatch fixed in the
spawnllm-0.5 / cc-transcript-7 migration, cc-pushback 0.7.0).

See [[cc-pushback-two-stage-pipeline]].
